name: Yahya ibn Umm al-Tiwal
title: The Steadfast Companion
shortDescription: One of the few loyal companions of Imam Sajjad (AS) during the oppressive era after Ashura, eventually martyred under torture by Hajjaj.
fullStory: Yahya ibn Umm al-Tiwal was a special and devoted companion of Imam Sajjad (AS). Following the tragedy of Karbala, when fear gripped the society, he was among the very few who maintained his connection with the Imam. He would courageously stand in the Mosque of Kufa, publicly disassociating from the Umayyads and the enemies of the Ahl al-Bayt. Eventually, Hajjaj ibn Yusuf arrested him. When Yahya refused to curse Imam Ali (AS), Hajjaj ordered his limbs to be amputated, leading to his martyrdom.
quote: We disassociate ourselves from you and what you worship instead of Allah.
